docs.title: PhD Candidate in Nuclear and Particle Physics
docs.text: Personal Website | LinkedIn | csoneira@ucm.es

docs.title: Welcome
docs.text:
I am a PhD candidate in Nuclear and Particle Physics at the Complutense University of Madrid, specializing in radiation detectors, cosmic ray studies, and medical imaging applications. My research focuses on designing and optimizing advanced detector technologies for both fundamental physics and real-world applications.

This website serves as a hub for my publications, conference contributions, research projects, and outreach activities.

docs.title: Explore My Work
docs.text:
• Research & Publications – Papers and ongoing projects
  • Conferences & Talks – Scientific presentations
  • Science Outreach – Public engagement initiatives

Feel free to explore and reach out if you’d like to collaborate.

docs.title: Professional Experience
docs.text:
• Researcher, Complutense University of Madrid (Jun 2023 – Present) Project New Generation of Molecular Imaging Devices for Breast Cancer (NeWMBI). Development of radiation detectors and Resistive Plate Chambers for cosmic-ray monitoring and medical imaging.

  • Predoctoral Fellow, Institute of Physics of Cantabria, CSIC (2022 – 2023) Work on advanced detector technologies and supervision of the JAE Intro ICU scholarship program.

docs.title: Education
docs.text:
• PhD in Nuclear and Particle Physics (in progress), Complutense University of Madrid (2023 – Present)
  • Master’s in Particle Physics and the Cosmos, University of Cantabria (2022 – 2023) Supported by the Spanish Nuclear Society Scholarship and CSIC JAE Intro Scholarship.
  • Double Degree in Physics and Mathematics, University of Santiago de Compostela (2017 – 2022)

docs.title: Publications
docs.text:

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
I am a predoctoral researcher at the Complutense University of Madrid, specializing in Nuclear and Particle Physics with applications in medical imaging and cosmic-ray instrumentation.

My research interests include electronic instrumentation, Resistive Plate Chambers and detector systems for both fundamental physics and clinical environments.

docs.title: Research Trajectory 🚀
docs.text:
My research focuses on Nuclear and Particle Physics, with applications in medical imaging, radiation detectors, and cosmic ray studies. Over the years, I have contributed to various publications, conferences, research projects, and science outreach initiatives.

Explore the different sections below to learn more about my academic and research journey:

docs.title: 🔹 Sections in this Category
docs.text:
• Publications – Peer-reviewed journal articles and submitted works.
  • Conferences – Presentations, talks, and posters at international and national meetings.
  • Outreach & Science Communication – Efforts to engage the public with science.
  • Research Projects – Key projects I have been involved in, including detector development.

This section provides a structured overview of my contributions to the field. Click on any topic to explore in detail!

docs.title: NeWMBI Project (2022 - 2025)
docs.text:
• Development of radiation detectors for medical imaging
  • Role: Team member, contributing to detector design and optimization

docs.title: miniTRASGO Cosmic Ray Telescope (2022 - Present)
docs.text:
• Design and deployment of a global network of Resistive Plate Chambers for cosmic-ray monitoring
  • Role: Detector development and data analysis

docs.title: In-beam PET for Proton Therapy (2024 - Present)
docs.text:
• Real-time dose reconstruction using positron emission tomography during treatment
  • Role: Algorithm development and detector simulation
